analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: float32 (TF32 tensorop path via cutlass_80 s1688gemm)

bottleneck.description: The 1x1 pointwise convolutions are lowered to FP32 TF32-tensorop cuTLASS GEMMs compiled for sm_80 (Ampere), running on a Blackwell sm_120 device. They are simultaneously occupancy-starved and Tensor-Core-underutilized: each block needs 140-228 registers/thread, capping achieved_occupancy at 8.2-8.5% (sm__warps_active ~8.4% of peak) and eligible_cycles_pct at 16-20%, which on Blackwell (warp_cycles_per_instruction unavailable) is the latency-bound signal (<20%). tensor_core_active_pct is only 19-45% because the s1688 path is FP32-accumulate TF32 on stale Ampere tiles. The 128x128 variant (k_00030/k_00069/k_00043/k_00082, dominant kernel of the prologue) is the single most expensive kernel in the model at ~38-39 us each.

transformation.description: Promote the pointwise (1x1) convolution inputs/weights from FP32 to bfloat16 (or fp16) before lowering. This halves DRAM traffic on the M=50176 x K/N GEMMs and, critically, routes Inductor's conv-as-mm lowering to a bf16 tensor-core GEMM template that Inductor can autotune for sm_120 instead of falling back to the prebuilt sm_80 s1688 (TF32) cuTLASS kernel. A bf16 epilogue also drops register pressure (the 224 regs/thread that pin occupancy at 8%), raising achieved occupancy and Tensor-Core active fraction. Pair with torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_gemm so the GEMM template is retuned for Blackwell rather than reusing the Ampere kernel.
transformation.location: Aten IR: aten.convolution.default nodes whose weight shape is [Cout, Cin, 1, 1] (the pointwise convs), i.e. the nodes feeding the cutlass s1688gemm kernels.
fx_steps: weight = node.args[1]  # the [Cout, Cin, 1, 1] pointwise filter
transformation.code_hint: Register as a torch._inductor.compile_fx pre-grad pass (torch._inductor.config.pre_grad_custom_pass) and set torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_gemm=True so the bf16 GEMM is autotuned for sm_120. Prefer a single up-front cast of the activation rather than per-conv casts; consecutive pointwise/depthwise stages can stay in bf16 to avoid round-trips.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~50% on pointwise-GEMM operands (FP32->bf16); the four large s1688gemm classes read 12.9-25.9 MB and write up to 134 MB each in FP32.

optimizations.notes: Medium confidence: changes numerics (FP32->bf16 inference); MCP/exa unavailable so novel-transform cap applies. The kernel name 'cutlass_80_...' confirms an Ampere-tuned kernel on Blackwell, which is why TC active is low despite the work being GEMM-shaped. Must run first: subsequent passes that register folded weight/bias buffers (OPT-2) allocate them at the runtime dtype and cannot be recast afterward.

bottleneck.description: BatchNorm is in inference form (_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training) but is NOT folded into the preceding convolution. Inductor lowered each BN+ReLU6 to a standalone memory-bound Triton kernel (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_norm_legit_no_training_hardtanh_*) because the conv went to a cuDNN/cuTLASS library call that cannot accept a Triton epilogue. These kernels do nothing but read the conv output from DRAM, apply a per-channel affine + clamp(0,6), and write it back. They run at 42-85% DRAM throughput with tensor_core_active_pct=0 and sm_throughput 12-31% -- pure bandwidth. The hardtanh_4 variant on the 256-channel output writes 165-472 MB at ~84% of peak DRAM BW. In inference, BN is a static per-channel scale+shift that is mathematically foldable into conv weight (w' = w * gamma/sqrt(var+eps)) and bias, eliminating the entire read-modify-write pass.

transformation.description: Conv-BN folding: rewrite each (aten.convolution.default -> aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default) chain by baking the BN affine into the conv weight and bias at trace time, then deleting the BN node. This removes every standalone BN+ReLU6 Triton kernel and the BN portion fused inside the prologue. The folded weight/bias are stored as new constant buffers (register_buffer), so this pass MUST run after the dtype promotion (OPT-1) so the buffers are created at the bf16 runtime dtype. ReLU6 (hardtanh) remains and is fused as a conv/GEMM epilogue in OPT-4.
transformation.location: Aten IR: matched pairs of aten.convolution.default immediately followed (in its users) by aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default.
fx_steps: if conv.target is not torch.ops.aten.convolution.default: continue
if len(conv.users) != 1: continue  # conv output consumed only by this BN
gamma, beta, mean, var = bn.args[1], bn.args[2], bn.args[3], bn.args[4]; eps = bn.args[-1]
fold scale/weight/bias into new get_attr constant buffers via gm.register_buffer(...)
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent to Inductor's freezing fuse_conv_bn / torch.fx.experimental.optimization.fuse_conv_bn_eval. Enable via torch._inductor.config.freezing=True (which runs conv+bn folding in the post-grad freezing pass for inference) plus the pre-grad custom pass for any convs that escape freezing. Numerically exact for inference (no_training BN).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates a full read+write of every conv output for the BN pass (up to 472 MB written on the 256-channel stage); folded affine costs zero runtime memory traffic.

optimizations.notes: High confidence: conv-BN folding in inference is numerically exact and a well-established Inductor freezing pass. Depends on OPT-1 only for the register_buffer dtype ordering rule. The standalone aten::_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training operator (12 kernels, 13.4%) disappears entirely; the BN math previously fused in the prologue Triton kernels also collapses.

bottleneck.description: The network input arrives NCHW but every conv kernel runs NHWC (the depthwise kernel is literally named conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc and the GEMMs want channels-last activations). Inductor inserts triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 permute/copy kernels (grid [98,16,1]) to relayout the tensor. These are pure data-movement: l1_hit_rate 0%, l2_hit_rate ~0.4%, sm_throughput 9-10%, tensor_core 0%, achieved_occupancy 52-58% -- a streaming transpose with no reuse. The aten::convolution_0 operator (20000 ns, 2 layout kernels) and several triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 instances inside the prologue exist only to fix layout at block boundaries.

transformation.description: Convert the whole module to channels_last (NHWC) once at the graph boundary so that the conv/GEMM kernels consume their native layout without per-block permute kernels. With a single input permute hoisted to the model entry and all weights pre-converted to channels_last, Inductor's layout-optimization pass propagates NHWC through the depthwise/pointwise chain and the intermediate triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 relayout copies fold away. Apply after OPT-1 so the channels_last weights are materialized at the bf16 runtime dtype.
transformation.location: Aten IR: the aten.convolution.default nodes and the inserted contiguous/clone (as_strided) nodes that realize NCHW<->NHWC at block boundaries; plus the model input placeholder.
fx_steps: model = model.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last)  # converts conv weights to NHWC strides
# at graph entry, permute the placeholder once:
inp = next(n for n in gm.graph.nodes if n.op == 'placeholder')
# rely on torch._inductor.config.layout_optimization to remove now-redundant interior permutes
transformation.code_hint: Set torch._inductor.config.layout_optimization=True (default on) and feed x = x.to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) at the call site; the model.to(channels_last) handles weights. Verify the interior triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 copies disappear in the Inductor debug output.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes redundant full-tensor transpose copies (each streams the activation through DRAM with ~0% cache reuse) at every layout-change boundary.

optimizations.notes: Medium confidence: layout propagation interacts with Inductor's autotuner and the exact set of surviving permutes is autotune-dependent; depends on OPT-1 for weight dtype. The depthwise kernel is already NHWC, so the win is purely eliminating boundary relayouts, not changing the conv kernels themselves.

bottleneck.description: After conv-BN folding (OPT-2), the depthwise 3x3 convs (conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc) still write their full output to DRAM and the ReLU6 (hardtanh) is applied by a separate Triton kernel reading it back. The depthwise kernels are memory-bound (dram_throughput 57-75%, sm_throughput 31-39%, tensor_core 0%, occupancy 74-86%); the trailing hardtanh kernels add another full read+write. Because the depthwise conv currently lowers to a library/ATen kernel, Inductor cannot fuse the clamp(0,6) epilogue into it.

transformation.description: Force the depthwise convolution to lower to an Inductor Triton conv template (rather than the ATen conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc library kernel) so the ReLU6/hardtanh epilogue (and, post-OPT-2, the folded bias add) fuses into the same kernel, eliminating the separate hardtanh read+write pass. Depthwise convs are cheap and Triton-expressible; epilogue fusion removes one full-tensor DRAM round-trip per depthwise stage.
transformation.location: Aten IR: aten.convolution.default with grouped weight [C,1,3,3] (groups==C) followed by aten.hardtanh.default (the ReLU6 clamp).
fx_steps: # enable Triton conv lowering + epilogue fusion in Inductor config (no manual node surgery needed):
# optionally pattern-match (conv -> hardtanh) to assert fusion in the post-grad pass:
if conv.target is torch.ops.aten.convolution.default and conv.args[8] == get_groups(conv):
mark conv for triton-template lowering (tag node meta so the scheduler fuses the clamp epilogue)
transformation.code_hint: Primarily a config-level change: torch._inductor.config.max_autotune + max_autotune_conv_backends='TRITON' lets the scheduler fuse the hardtanh (and folded bias from OPT-2) into the depthwise conv epilogue. Validate against the Inductor debug dir that conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc is replaced by a fused triton conv kernel.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full-tensor read+write per depthwise stage by fusing the ReLU6 (and folded bias) epilogue into the conv kernel.

optimizations.notes: Medium confidence (capped: novel-ish transform, exa-search unavailable). Must run after OPT-2 -- the ReLU6 epilogue can only fuse into the conv once BN has been folded out of the graph; otherwise BN sits between conv and hardtanh and blocks epilogue fusion. Triton depthwise conv may not always beat the library kernel; gate behind autotune so the faster path is selected.

global_notes: 4 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 2.93% of attributed time): aten::cudnn_convolution op_id=41 (0.90%), op_id=14 (0.89%), op_id=6 (0.57%), op_id=33 (0.56%).

hardware-limits.md was not found at the expected path; sm_count and ridge-point for 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition' were taken from general Blackwell GB202 knowledge (assumed ~188 SMs). Wave/SM and ridge-point figures are approximate. ASSUMPTION FLAGGED.

fx-patterns.md was not found and MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were unavailable. Per the fallback policy, FX API usage relies on built-in knowledge and novel transforms are capped at 'medium' confidence; conv-BN folding (OPT-2) remains 'high' as a standard, numerically-exact inference pass. No output was withheld due to tool unavailability.

Blackwell removes warp_cycles_per_instruction (null throughout the profile); latency-bound detection used eligible_cycles_pct < 20. The cuTLASS s1688gemm pointwise kernels show eligible_cycles_pct 16-20% and achieved_occupancy ~8%, confirming they are latency/occupancy-bound.

edge_case_flag fused_kernel_double_count: nearly all operators have is_fused=true and the profile contains both an inductor-compiled 'layer::unique::prologue' aggregate (33 kernels) AND separate per-block aten::cudnn_convolution / aten::_native_batch_norm operators covering the same forward pass. These overlap; absolute durations and the sum-based time budget should be read as relative weights, not additive wall-clock. duration_ns is also 2-5x inflated by ncu counter collection -- used only for in-profile relative comparison.

edge_case_flag warm_up_inflation: capture_metadata has no measure_iters field, so kernel-count/iteration multiples could not be verified; the prologue NVTX range appears twice (two start_ns) suggesting >1 measured iteration captured into one aggregate. Flagged as a caveat on kernel_count interpretation.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (dtype) -> OPT-2 (conv-BN fold, register_buffer at runtime dtype) and -> OPT-3 (channels_last weights at runtime dtype) and -> OPT-4. OPT-2 -> OPT-4 (ReLU6 epilogue fusion requires BN already folded out). No cycles. Apply order: OPT-1, OPT-2, OPT-3, OPT-4.

Root cause summary: the model is dominated by (a) FP32 1x1-pointwise convs running on Ampere-tuned (cutlass_80) s1688 GEMMs at ~8% occupancy on a Blackwell device, and (b) unfused, memory-bound BatchNorm+ReLU6 Triton passes that exist because the convs went to library kernels. dtype promotion + Blackwell autotune addresses (a); conv-BN folding + epilogue fusion addresses (b).
